original: Honoratus, first known Prefect of the City of Constantinople, takes office, after the Emperor Constantius creates a new senate for the East
St Damasus I dies and ends his reign as Catholic Pope. Began reign 366
K'inich Yax K'uk Mo', founding ruler of Maya city of Copán, marks completion of an important calendrical cycle under Maya Long Count calendar (Stela 63). Oldest known inscription from Copán.

John "Eleanor" Rykener, a male cross-dressing prostitute, is brought to court in London for "committing that detestable unmentionable and ignominious vice" in late medieval England's only recorded case on same-sex intercourse (verdict unknown)
Heretic Nicolaas Serrurier exiled from Florence
Duchess Maria of Burgundy ends the 'Great Privilege' in states of The Netherlands
Spanish troops begin siege of Haarlem
The Polish–Lithuanian Commonwealth and the Tsardom of Russia agree to the Truce of Deulino ending the Polish–Muscovite War (1605–1618)
"Messiah" Sjabtai Tswi festival in Smyrna

Second Battle of Tobago: French forces under Jean II d'Estrées defeat the Dutch led by Jacob Binckes, who is killed along with many of his men when a French shell detonates the defenders' powder magazine, destroying the fort and ending Dutch military power in the Antilles
Battle of Villa Viciosa: France beats the Habsburgers
1st recorded display of Aurora Borealis in north American colonies (New England)
1st newspaper on Curacao (Curacao Gazette & Commercial Advertiser)
Citizens of Geneva thwart Savoyard invaders
Indiana becomes 19th state of the Union
1st dental use of nitrous oxide in Hartford, Connecticut
1st yacht race across Atlantic Ocean; schooners Fleetwing, Vesta and the Henrietta, with NY playboy Gordon Bennett on board, leave Sandy Hook for the Scilly Isles (the Henrietta wins)
Pinckney Benton Stewart Pinchback takes office in Louisiana as the first African American US Governor
French Panama Canal Company fails
11 fishing ships wash up at Wadden Sea, 22 killed
The US signs a treaty with Cuba allowing for a 20 percent reduction of tariff rates on imported Cuban products
British forces under MacDonald & Younghusband march into Tibet
A workers uprising occurs, establishing the Shuliavka Republic in Kiev
British government of Campbell-Bannerman forms
High temperature of 120°F (49°C) recorded in Rivadavia, Argentina, a record for all of South America
New Zealand Parliament Buildings almost completely destroyed by fire
Frederick Delius' orchestral work "In a Summer Garden" premieres in London
Canadian Football exhibition game played in Van Cortlandt Park in Bronx, Hamilton Tigers beat Ottawa Rough Riders, 11-6 before 15,000
Colored moving pictures demonstrated at Madison Square Garden, NYC
"Mona Lisa" recovered two years after it was stolen from the Louvre Museum
Stockton Street Tunnel (San Francisco) completed
13 black soldiers hanged for participation in Houston riot
German-occupied Lithuania proclaims independence from Russia
Boll weevil monument dedicated in Enterprise, Alabama
Bank of the United States closes in New York City
Japan leaves the Gold Standard
Statute of Westminster gives complete legislative independence to Canada, Australia, New Zealand, South Africa, Ireland (Free State), and Newfoundland (not then part of Canada)
San Francisco's coldest day (27°F) - it snows
1935 All-Star Game is assigned to Cleveland
1st successful penalty shot by a Toronto Maple Leaf - Charlie Conacher stopped by New York Rangers Andy Aitkenhead at Madison Square Garden
1st Toronto Maple Leaf penalty shot, Conacher unsuccessful vs Rangers
Ford C. Frick becomes president of baseball's National League
NL votes to permit night baseball (up to 7 games per home team)
Italy withdraws from League of Nations
Axis powers Nazi Germany and Fascist Italy declare war against the United States
Dutch government in London declares war on Italy
Japanese attack Wake Island (only failed WW II-landing)
Japanese occupy Guam
NY Giants acquire future Baseball Hall of Fame first baseman Johnny Mize from Cardinals for 3 players & $50,000
Australian Dutch guerrilla troops evacuated to Timor near Australia
Surprise attack on House of Keeping Axe, 29 prisoners freed
Het Parool publishes 1st Captain Rob-strip
Spain suspended from UN
United Nations International Children's Emergency Fund (UNICEF) is established by resolution 57(I) of the UN General Assembly
Pacific Coast League application for major league status rejected
WHEN (now WTVH) TV channel 5 in Syracuse, NY (CBS) begins broadcasting
WMC TV channel 5 in Memphis, Tennessee (NBC) begins broadcasting
Chicago Bear Johnny Lujack passes for 6 touchdowns vs Chicago Cards (52-29)
WOAI (now KMOL) TV channel 4 in San Antonio, TX (NBC) 1st broadcast
Baseball owners vote 9-7 not to renew Commissioner Chandler's contract
KTVA TV channel 11 in Anchorage (CBS) becomes Alaska's 1st TV station
Philadelphia Phillies owners reluctantly purchase Connie Mack Stadium (Shibe Park) from the A's
USS Forrestal aircraft carrier christened in Newport News, Virginia
Anti-Russian demonstrations in Stettin & Wroclaw, Poland
4th (last) Dutch government of Willem Drees falls
Upper Volta (now Burkina Faso) gains autonomy from France
Black Sunday - Riot in Algiers; pro-independence demonstrations turn violent, 114 die
Cleveland Brown's Bernie Parrish sets club record for longest interception return with a 92 yard run
Motown Records releases "Please, Mr. Postman" by the Marvelettes; it is the group and the label's 1st #1 pop song
Al Nelson sets NFL record returning missed field goal, 100 yards
6.6 earthquake in Koynanagar, India, kills at least 177, injures over 2,000 and lightly damages the Koyna Dam
People's Front for the Liberation of Palestine (PFLP) established
Supersonic airliner prototype "Concorde" 1st shown (France)
The Beatles' Apple Music signs its first group - Grapefruit
KECC (now KECY) TV channel 9 in El Centro, CA (CBS) 1st broadcast
US Soccer Football Association refuses to let NASL disband
Libya adopts constitution
Start of the 1st Test match at the WACA, Australia v England
A bomb explodes outside a furniture showroom on the mainly-Protestant and loyalist Shankill Road, Belfast; four civilians (including two babies) were killed and nineteen wounded
The Libertarian Party of the United States is formed
New York Jets' Don Maynard becomes all time pro reception leader (632)
Houston Astro César Cedeño jailed for the death of a 19-year-old woman
NA Soccer League awards LA, San Francisco, Seattle & Vancouver franchises
US 1st class postage rises from 10 cents to 13 cents

Mairead Corrigan and Betty Williams, leaders of the group 'Peace People' (an organization dedicated to encouraging a peaceful resolution of 'the Troubles' in Northern Ireland) receive the Nobel Peace Prize

6 masked men bound 10 employees at Lufthansa cargo area at NYC Kennedy Airport and made off with $5.8M in cash & jewelry; all of the participants and some associates were later killed on order of  the alleged organized crime mastermind, loot never recovered
NBC premiere of "A Woman Called Moses"
Geoff Boycott scores cricket century in a limited-over international
The Comprehensive Environmental Response, Compensation, and Liability Act (known as either CERCLA or Superfund) is enacted by the U.S. Congress.
Argentine President General Roberto Viola flees
El Mozote massacre: Salvadoran armed forces kill an estimated 900 civilians in an anti-guerrilla campaign during the country's civil war.
Spacelab I arrives at Kennedy Space Center
UN Security Council chooses Javier Pérez de Cuélla of Peru as the 5th Secretary-General of the United Nations
Wash Capitals biggest margin of victory (9) beating Toronto 11-2
Mauretania military coup under Col Maawiya Ould Sid'ahmed Taya
Computer store owner in Sacramento California killed by package bomb
Dow Jones Industrial Average closes above 1,500 for 1st time (1,511.70)
General Electric acquires RCA Corp & its subsidiary, NBC
NHL Record 62 points scored, Edmonton (36) beats Chicago (26) 12-9 & ties record of 21 goals
South Africa censors press
Mark Davis signs record $3.25 million per year KC Royals contract
13 die in 83 vehicle accident in Chattanooga Tn I-75, due to fog
US 69th manned space mission STS 35 (Columbia 11) returns from space
William Kennedy Smith found not guilty of rape
Nor'easter storm hits NY, doing $ Billion worth of damage
WNEW AM radio on 1130 in NYC ends transmitting after 58 years
59th Heisman Trophy Award: Charlie Ward, Florida State (QB)
Eduardo Frei Ruiz-Tagle elected President of Chile
Russian Federation forces attack city of Grozny, beginning the First Chechen War, in which the Chechen Republic of Ichkeria gains its independence from Russia
Russian troops enter Chechnya
Thomas O Hicks buys NHL Dallas Stars for $84 million
Delegates from 150 industrial nations attending a UN climate conference in Kyoto, Japan, reach agreement to control heat-trapping greenhouse gases
Federal judge orders Microsoft not to bundle IE4 in Windows
Former Governor Edward D. DiPrete pleads guilty to 18 counts of bribery, extortion and racketeering
Thai Airways Airbus A310-200 crashes near Surat Thani Airport, killing 101
66th Heisman Trophy Award: Chris Weinke, Florida State (QB)
The People's Republic of China joins the World Trade Organization.
70th Heisman Trophy Award: Matt Leinart, USC (QB)

Cronulla riots: thousands of white Australians demonstrate against ethnic violence, resulting in a riot against anyone thought to be Lebanese (and many who were not) in Cronulla Sydney. These are followed by ethnic attacks on Cronulla.
The Buncefield Oil Depot in Hemel Hempstead, England, is rocked by explosions, causing a huge oil fire.
Two car bombs go off at the Constitutional Court building in Algiers and the United Nations office. An estimated 45 people are killed in the bombings.
Angry Birds, a Finnish puzzle video game released internationally
Two explosions occur in a busy shopping district of Stockholm, Sweden, killing one person and injuring two others. Europol categorizes the attack as terrorism
Mosconi Cup nine-ball pool, Las Vegas, Nevada: Team Europe retains trophy with an 11-7 win over US; MVP: Niels Feijen (Netherlands)
125 people are killed and 200 are injured by bombings in Aqrab, Syria
HSBC bank settles with US authorities to pay $1.9 billion for drug cartel money laundering
20 people are killed by the bubonic plague in a small Madagascan village
World's 1st penis transplant procedure by a team from Stellenbosch University and Tygerberg Hospital in Cape Town, South Africa
Unrest in Burundi leads to clashes between authorities and protesters in Bujumbura, killing at least 87
Bombing at a chapel in Cairo, Egypt, kills 25 and wounds 45
Kyrgyzstan votes in a referendum to change the constitution to give the government more power
Attempted suicide terrorist bomb attack in New York wounds 3, bomb fails to fully detonate
Landmark trial for Huntington's disease in London announces positive results - first time a drug has been able to slow an incurable brain disorder
Rahul Gandhi, son of former Prime Minister Rajiv Gandhi, is elected leader of the Indian National Congress
Saudi Arabia announces an end to its 35 year ban on cinemas
Six wildfires in Southern California now cover area larger than New York City and Boston combined
The Arctic is experiencing "unprecedented warmth" caused by human-caused climate change, according to US National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration Report
Bougainville votes to become independent of Papua New Guinea in a referendum
Discovery of the earliest figurative artwork in the world, (43,900 years old), figures part human part animal, from cave in Sulawesi, Indonesia, reported in journal "Nature"
India passes controversial Citizenship Amendment Bill clearing way for citizenship for immigrants from Pakistan, Bangladesh and Afghanistan but not if they are Muslim
Militants attack army base in Ates, Niger, killing at least 71. Islamic State claim responsibility.
America's FDA authorizes the Pfizer/BioNTech COVID-19 vaccine for emergency use
European Union leaders agree to cut net carbon emissions by 55% in the next decade
First same-sex civil union registered in Bolivia after a two-year legal battle by activists
Gunmen storm a school in Kankara, Katsina state, northern Nigeria, kidnapping more than 300 students (Boko Haram later claims responsibility)
87th Heisman Trophy Award: Bryce Young, Alabama (QB)
Marc Shaiman and Scott Wittman's musical "Some Like It Hot", based on the 1959 feature film comedy, opens at the Shubert Theatre, NYC; garners 13 Tony Award nominations
NASA's Orion spacecraft returns to earth after completing Artemis I test flight around the moon in 25.5 days - record distance traveled by a spacecraft designed to carry humans
Canadian accused poison seller Kenneth Law who allegedly sent 200 packages to people in more than 40 countries, is charged with 14 counts of murder
Former PM and European Council President, Donald Tusk elected Prime Minister of Poland at head of a coalition government, after eight years of controversial conservative rule

Llywelyn ab Gruffydd the last native and independent Prince of Wales is killed at the Battle of Orewin Bridge at Cilmeri, near Builth Wells, south Wales by forces ofEdward Iof England. Reigned from 1259 and is known as Llywelyn the Last.
Surprise attack by forces of the Duke of Savoy and his brother-in-law,Philip IIIof Spain repelled by citizens of Geneva. Commemorated since as Fête de l'Escalade.
KingJames IIcaptured in Kent, after trying to flee to France upon the arrival of William of Orange
Voltaire's tragedy "Brutus" premieres in Paris
French KingLouis XVIgoes on trial, accused of high treason and crimes against the state
Battle of Fredricksburg in Virginia begins betweenRobert E. Lee's Confederate Army of Northern Virginia and the Union Army of the Potomac, commanded by Major GeneralAmbrose Burnside
Boston's Bijou Theatre, 1st American playhouse lit exclusively by electricity holds its 1st performance,W. S. GilbertandArthur Sullivan's comic opera "Iolanthe"
Victorien Sardous play "Fedora" withSarah Bernhardtpremieres in Paris
US PresidentTheodore Rooseveltattacks abuses in the Congo
David Lloyd Georgeforms British war government
PopePius XIpublishes encyclical Quas Primas
Buenos Aires police thwart assassination attempt on President-electHerbert Hoover- prevent anarchists from bombing his train
Russian generalGeorgy Zhukovwarns of German assault
Hank Williamsbegins to record for the Sterling Records label
British PhysicistCecil Frank Powellawarded Nobel Prize in Physics for his study of nuclear processes and the discovery of the pion
Paul Hindemith's Concerto for Clarinet premieres with Philadelphia Orchestra conducted byEugene Ormandy, andBenny Goodmanas soloist, at the Academy of Music
"The Wild Side of Life" single recorded byHank Thompsonand His Brazos Valley Boys (Billboard Song of the Year, 1952)
Joe DiMaggioannounces his retirement from baseball
Archibald MacLeish's play "J.B." premieres in NYC
Emilio G. SegrèandOwen Chamberlainawarded Nobel Prize for Physics for discovery of the antiproton, a sub-atomic antiparticle
NY Yankees trade Marv Thronberry, Don Larsen,Hank Bauerand Norm Seibern forRoger Maris, Kent Hadley and Joe Deaestri
Cy Colemanand Carolyn Leigh's musical "Wildcat" withLucille Ballpremieres in New York City
Elvis Presley's "Blue Hawaii" album goes #1 and stays #1 for 20 weeks
PresidentJohn F. Kennedyprovides US military helicopters and crews to South Vietnam
"Time" magazine callsSusan Sontag"one of Manhattan's brightest intellectuals" in a review of her groundbreaking essay "Notes on Camp"
Che Guevaraspeaks at the United Nations General Assembly in New York City. An unknown terrorist fires a mortar shell at the building during the speech.
"Guess Who's Coming to Dinner", directed by Stanley Kramer, starringSpencer Tracy,Sidney PoitierandKatharine Hepburn, premieres in NYC (Hepburn - Academy Award for Best Actress 1968)
Northern Ireland Prime MinisterTerence O'Neillsacks Home Affairs Minister, William Craig
West German chancellorWilly Brandtnormalizes trade with Czechoslovakia
MLB New York Yankees make great trade gettingWillie Randolph, Dock Ellis &Ken Brettfrom Pittsburgh Pirates for George "Doc" Medich
"Magnum P.I." starringTom Selleckpremieres on CBS
Former world heavyweight boxing championMuhammad Ali's 61st and last fight; loses toTrevor Berbickby unanimous decision in 10 rounds at Queen Elizabeth Sports Centre, Nassau, Bahamas
1st visit to Lutheran church by a pope (John Paul IIin Rome)
"Wall Street" film directed byOliver Stoneand starringMichael DouglasandCharlie Sheenis released
Martin Ritt's dramatic film "Nuts", starringBarbra Streisandand Richard Dreyfus premieres
"Hook", a swashbuckling film sequel to the Peter Pan story, directed bySteven Spielberg, and starringRobin WilliamsandDustin Hoffmanpremieres
US President George H.W. Bush awards actress and humanitarianAudrey Hepburnthe Presidential Medal of Freedom
Joe Sakicscores his 500th career goal at Vancouver
International Conference to Review the Global Vision of the Holocaust is opened in Tehran, Iran byMahmoud Ahmadinejad
Jerry Sloanachieves his 1,000th career win as a coach against the Dallas Mavericks
Bernie Madoffarrested and charged with securities fraud in $50 billion Ponzi scheme
Tiger Woodsannounces an indefinite leave from professional golf to focus on his marriage.
Jeopardy! hostAlex Trebeksuffers a minor heart attack in his home and is admitted to Cedars-Sinai Medical Center
British physicist,Stephen Hawking, wins the $3 million Fundamental Physics Prize, the most lucrative academic prize in the world
Pope Francisis named Time magazine's person of the year
CIA DirectorJohn Brennandefends interrogation methods used after 9/11 but admits some methods were "abhorrent"
"Playboy" magazine publishes its last nude issue, featuresPamela Andersonon the cover
Gambian leaderYahya Jammehsays he will contest his election defeat in court, after his 1st election loss in 22 years
US Senator Kirsten Gillibrand calls for the resignation of PresidentDonald Trumpgiven 16 women have accused him of sexual harassment
Time Magazine Person of the Year 2018 is "the Guardians" journalists targeted for their work, includingJamal Khashoggi
Climate activistGreta Thunbergis named Time magazine's Person of the Year
US Supreme Court rejects lawsuit by Texas to overturn JoBiden's election in four battleground states, endorsed by 17 Republican attorney generals
Brock Purdywins his first career start against the Tampa Bay Buccaneers
Karen Bass sworn in as first female mayor of Los Angeles by Vice PresidentKamala Harris, declares a Homelessness state of emergency in the city
Mayflower Pilgrims come ashore in Plymouth Bay, traditionally thought to be at Plymouth Rock, Massachusetts [NS: Dec 21]
Anglo-Zulu War: British high commissioner Henry Bartle Frere presents an ultimatum to the Zulu Kingdom to submit to British rule or face war
Chief Electrical Engineer of the British Post Office, William Preece, gives a public lecture in London called "Telegraphy without Wires", praising the work of 22-year-oldGuglielmo Marconi
Edward VIII announces in a radio broadcast that he is abdicating the British throne to marryWallis Simpson
US authorities announce that the Libyan man accused of making the bomb on board Pan Am Flight 103, Abu Agila Mas’ud, is now in US custody
